item.title: Amazon’s Jassy Sees Company’s Breadth as a Strength
item.description: Few companies serve as many consumers and businesses

item.title: Warsh to Be Sworn in as Fed Chief During White House Ceremony
item.description: Kevin Warsh, who’s promised the biggest shakeup in decades at the US central bank, is set to be sworn into office Friday in a White House ceremony as the 17th chair of the Federal Reserve.

item.title: Reeves’ Summer Discounts Give BOE a Helping Hand, Analysis Shows
item.description: Chancellor of the Exchequer Rachel Reeves’ plans to help families cope with the cost of living this summer could also help the Bank of England, according to Bloomberg economists.

item.title: OHB and KNDS Weigh Tweaks to Share Sale Timetables to Avoid SpaceX
item.description: Two companies planning major share sales in Europe are considering pushing back the launches of their deals to avoid the expected market melee around SpaceX’s record initial public offering in the US.

item.title: India RBI to Pay Record Dividend, Misses Market Estimates
item.description: India’s central bank will transfer 2.87 trillion rupees ($30 billion) to the government, a record but one that fell short of expectations even as the Iran war pushes up energy prices and strains public finances.

item.title: This Wouldn’t Be Much of a Rally Without AI Stocks
item.description: Equal-weighted S&P 500 is unchanged since war broke out.

item.title: China’s AI-Made Video Is Changing the Entertainment Landscape
item.description: Such services pose an existential threat to traditional entertainment.

item.title: The $4.50 Gas Economy: Fewer Nights Out, More Belt-Tightening
item.description: Higher prices at the pump are making Avarisse Crawford rethink her spending. The 33-year-old has started scaling back her “fun budget,” meaning fewer steak dinners and happy hours with coworkers — instead she’s seeking out free activities, like going to the park.

item.title: Zara Owner Inditex’s CEO Bets on Diversification, AI for Growth
item.description: Zara owner Inditex SA is banking on diversification across brands and countries as well as artificial intelligence to spur growth at the world’s largest listed retailer, Chief Executive Officer Óscar García Maceiras said.

item.title: Buyout Bankers Go Big on Debt Pre-Sales to Thwart Fickle Markets
item.description: Timing can make or break a leveraged debt sale. Get it right, and banks secure cheap financing for their client and maximize fees. Get it wrong, and they can end up with their profits — and reputation — on the line.

item.title: Economists Boost US Inflation Forecast, Push Out Fed Cut on War
item.description: Economists raised their US inflation estimates and pushed out their timeline for the next Federal Reserve interest-rate cut as the price shock triggered by the Iran war starts to spread beyond higher energy costs.

item.title: Superbonus Legacy Is Making Italy’s Debt Stand Out, BE Says
item.description: Italy’s increasing debt burden risks mounting scrutiny from investors despite the efforts of Giorgia Meloni’s government to bring it under control, according to Bloomberg Economics.

item.title: Samsung Chip Workers Face Colleagues’ Resentment Over Bonus Deal
item.description: Samsung Electronics Co. staved off a potentially catastrophic strike this week, reaching a tentative deal with leaders of its largest union at the last minute to share more profits from the AI boom. But inside the $1.1 trillion technology behemoth, not everyone is happy.

item.title: ECB Chief Says Inflation Expectations Remain On Target
item.description: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde stressed that long-term inflation expectations are still broadly in line with the 2% target, despite deepening fallout from the Iran war.

item.title: Gilts Set for Best Week Gain Since 2023 as Rates Offset Politics
item.description: After starting the week at their highest in almost three decades, yields on long-dated gilts are poised for their biggest drop in more than two years.

item.title: Euro-Zone Wage Growth Slowed Before War Drove Inflation Up
item.description: A key measure of euro-area pay growth slowed, providing some relief to European Central Bank officials worrying about emerging inflation risks from the Iran war.

item.title: Salesforce Touts AI Promise Over Reality in SaaSpocalypse Fight
item.description: Patients at the University of Chicago Medicine featured in a promotional video seamlessly refill prescriptions, book appointments and even get parking tips with the help of Agentforce, Salesforce Inc.’s flagship AI tool.

item.title: London Traders Are Stuck With an Ultra-Long Bond Bet Gone Bad
item.description: It was supposed to be a relatively quick and lucrative bet. Buy a long-dated UK government bond at an historic discount to par, wait for the price to pop and then reap a tax-free windfall.

item.title: China Cracks Down on Illegal Cross-Border Securities Trading
item.description: China is launching a sweeping campaign against illicit cross-border securities, futures and fund trading, in an unprecedented crackdown that aims to stamp out such activities within two years.

item.title: ECB Hike Likely in June to Signal Commitment to 2%, Demarco Says
item.description: The European Central Bank will likely raise interest rates next month to underline its commitment to deliver on its mandate, according to Governing Council member Alexander Demarco.

item.title: AI Cartoon ‘Critterz’ Misses Cannes Debut After OpenAI Shut Sora
item.description: Critterz, a feature-length cartoon intended to showcase how OpenAI’s video-generation capabilities could revolutionize filmmaking, has missed its planned Cannes Film Festival debut after the artificial intelligence company shut down its Sora tool, forcing its creators to look for a new AI partner.

item.title: China to Penalize Tiger, Futu in Cross-Border Flow Crackdown
item.description: China’s securities regulator plans to penalize Futu Holdings Ltd., UP Fintech Holding Ltd.’s Tiger Brokers and Longbridge Securities Ltd. for operating on the mainland without a license, marking a sharp escalation in a yearslong campaign against illicit cross-border brokerages.

item.title: Mol Shares Drop After Petrochemicals Plant Explosion in Hungary
item.description: Shares in Mol Nyrt., Hungary’s biggest energy group, dropped after an explosion at its petrochemicals plant in eastern Hungary killed one person and seriously injured several others.

item.title: Kenyan President Rules Out Further Fuel-Tax Cuts After Protests
item.description: Kenyan President William Ruto ruled out further cuts to fuel taxes, even after deadly protests over the high cost of diesel brought the nation’s capital to a standstill this week.

item.title: Total Explores Selling Stakes in European Green Assets
item.description: TotalEnergies SE is exploring selling a 50% stake in some of its European renewables assets, as the French energy giant seeks a partner for its green investments, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: ‘The Assassin’ Fahmi Quadir on How to Survive as a Short-Seller
item.description: Plus, a conversation about the Korean stock market

item.title: Short-Seller Dubbed ‘The Assassin’ Is Going Long Korea Stocks
item.description: One of the most prominent names among a shrinking group of short-sellers who make their money by betting against stocks has a new strategy: going long the world’s best-performing market.

item.title: Odd Lots: Fahmi Quadir on Surviving as a Short-Seller (Podcast)
item.description: A short-seller is a gum shoe who roots out a particular story about a specific company and brings it to light. In this way, a short-seller is similar to an investigative journalist. Fahmi Quadir, the founder and CIO of Safkhet Capital, is known as the Assassin. Her notoriety proceeds her: Among her most well-known bets include short-selling Wirecard AG and Valeant. In today’s conversation with Qaudir — recorded at our live show in London at Wilton’s Music Hall — she dishes on what life is like f

item.title: StanChart CEO Apologizes for ‘Lower-Value Human’ Comments
item.description: Standard Chartered Plc Chief Executive Officer Bill Winters apologized for his comments earlier this week about how artificial intelligence will affect “lower-value human capital,” after such remarks spurred public backlash.

item.title: China Restricts Chemical Exports to US in Fentanyl Crackdown
item.description: China imposed new export controls on some key chemical ingredients shipped to the US, Mexico and Canada, in a further sign of cooperation with Washington on curbing drug trafficking.
